solution.p: There are a few ways to solve quadratic equations- the easiest way to solve this particular type of problem is to use the square root method.

p.m: \displaystyle{ \begin{aligned} 13 - 3 ( r+4 )^2 \amp = 1 \\ 13 - 3 ( r+4 )^2 \mathbf{{}-13} \amp = 1 \mathbf{{}-13} \\ - 3 ( r+4 )^2 \amp = -12 \\ \frac{- 3 ( r+4 )^2}{-3} \amp = \frac{-12}{-3} \\ ( r+4 )^2 \amp = 4 \\ \sqrt{( r+4 )^2} \amp = \pm\sqrt{4} \\ r+4 \amp = \pm 2 \\ r+4 \mathbf{{}-4} \amp = \pm 2 \mathbf{{}-4} \\ r \amp = 2-4 \text{ or } r=-2-4 \\ r \amp = -2 \text{ or } r=-6 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: Checking solutions is left as exercise.
